title: Remove Unused Private Fields
description: A private field that is declared but not used locally is considered dead code due to its limited visibility. This could indicate that some logic is missing or that the code needs cleaning. Cleaning out dead code reduces the size of the maintained codebase, making it easier to comprehend and preventing bugs from being introduced.
recommendation: Review and remove any unused private fields in your code. Take note, this rule does not take reflection into account, thus, issues will be raised on private fields only accessed using the reflection API.
impact: Leaving unused fields in your code can increase the complexity and size of the codebase, making it harder to maintain and potentially leading to bugs.

checks.message: Review and remove any unused private fields in your code.
